mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held at an elevated level relative to its trailing 12-month average continues to compress equity valuation multiples through higher discounting of future cash flows, producing a persistent drag on S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year walk-forward window, or high-rate periods show statistically indistinguishable returns from low-rate periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month moving average of the unemployment rate rises 0.3 percentage points or more above its trailing 12-month low (a Sahm-rule-style threshold), it signals the onset of labor market deterioration that historically precedes recessionary equity drawdowns, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: 3-month MA of UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above trailing 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% as of t-1mo, near historical cycle lows
failure_condition: Following triggered instances, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the signal fails to precede an actual rise in UNRATE trend over the subsequent 6 months in more than half of historical trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between the level of the federal funds rate and the year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL determines the real policy rate; when nominal rates remain high while inflation is falling, the implied real rate rises sharply, tightening financial conditions and depressing S&P 500 returns over the next month more than either series would predict alone.
failure_condition: The joint real-rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY) shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS alone (interaction term coefficient not significant at p<0.10) across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and consumer confidence, but this effect on forward equity returns is only material during periods of restrictive monetary policy when housing is most sensitive to financing costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter specifically in tightening regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1256.0 thousand units as of t-2mo
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive real rates
failure_condition: Negative HOUST YoY declines during tightening regimes are not followed by below-median 60-day SPX forward returns in at least 55% of historical instances, or the effect disappears once conditioned on regime (interaction with FEDFUNDS level insignificant).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy supply-side momentum and corporate output expansion, which is quickly priced into equities over short horizons, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly growth and forward 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no short-horizon predictive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A payroll (PAYEMS) print that falls more than one standard deviation below its trailing 12-month average growth rate signals an abrupt labor demand shock that triggers rapid downward repricing of growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns within the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: monthly PAYEMS change falls more than 1 std dev below trailing 12-month average growth
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 150263.0 thousand as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Triggered weak-payroll events are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the effect is not distinguishable from random noise at p>0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 0.5% signals weakening consumer demand that has historically been an early warning indicator for equity market softness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 513531.0 million dollars as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Triggered retail sales contraction events are followed by positive 20-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of historical cases, invalidating the demand-weakness transmission mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of decelerating retail sales (RSAFS) growth and rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a self-reinforcing consumption-labor feedback loop that only becomes predictive of a quarterly equity downturn during late-cycle expansions when both demand and labor slack simultaneously deteriorate, whereas either signal alone is insufficient.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with simultaneous demand and labor softening
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-UNRATE interaction term shows no significant incremental explanatory power over either univariate signal (interaction coefficient p>0.10) across rolling 3-year walk-forward windows, or joint deterioration episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time.
